text: On mute, the classic Zoom mix up.
How are you doing?
Yeah, doing great.
I'm actually taking off tomorrow for my bachelor party, so a little tough to stay focused at the end of the day.
Wow, look at you.
Congrats.
Yeah, thanks so much.
Are you married yourself?
No, I'm not, but as so many of my friends, I have eight weddings and five bachelor parties this year.
so safe to say, starting to hit that phase of life.
mean, a lot of my friends are doing it, you know, just getting married.
So it's been, it's just fine this year.
That's definitely the theme of this year for me.
Where's the bachelor party?
Yeah, I feel like in my 20s, it would have been like Vegas or something.
But, you know, in my 30s, I just picked a place that I've been wanting to go to and haven't been.
We're going to Oregon.
We're going to check out the Columbia River Gorge.
We do some trails, buy waterfalls, and we're going to watch a  ton of college football at the Airbnb together.
I'll tell you what, that sounds awesome.

I mean, it was my birthday a couple weeks ago, and just got an Airbnb in Newport, Rhode Island, you know, with my brother, like, basically just a bunch of buddies, and it was just like a guy's weekend.
It wasn't like anything crazy, and you just kind of realize that that feels great, and this is going to be, you know, it's going to be an exciting weekend for it, so good for you.
And nice that you're doing it on, you know, nice that you can get Monday off, hopefully, for Labor Day, so you can kind of really stretch it out a little bit.
Oh, absolutely, and I went ahead and took Tuesday off as well to make sure I'm in tip-top shape come Wednesday.
Anyways, Marcel has been in, like, back-to-backs all day today, so we'll kick it off together, and he'll probably join pretty quick.
I know that Justin was, like, the intro here, but would love maybe just some initial context and an intro from yourself before we dive in.
Yeah, yeah.
No, I mean, I appreciate that.
So I'll give the quick background on fund.
mean, yeah, Justin and I, so I'm an early stage investor at RTP Global, you know, from a fund perspective where we are a global firm.
We have offices in New York, SF, London, Paris, Bangalore, Dubai, Amsterdam, Israel.
We've been around for over 27 years.
We're investing out of fund four.
It's a billion dollar vehicle.
We reserve more than half of that capital for follow-on rounds.
So we're, you know, true early stage investors.
We do like pre-seed to series A.
Our check size is, you know, one to 15 million.
And then we reserve the majority of our fund to double, triple, quadruple down the winners in the portfolio.
You know, best example of that is our investment in Datadog.
We were the first institutional money in the company, followed on basically every subsequent round.
We piled in as much cash as we could, which resulted in us being the second largest shareholder at the time of IPO.
And so that's, you know, really fundamentally what we're trying to replicate.
And so we were, you know, we got in touch with Justin.
Obviously he was at Datadog.
You know, we, I reached out to him, we got connected about, you know, potentially investing in his company.
He's a great guy, we, you know, we built a relationship, we, you know, run on, you know, in New York together and everything.
And so he just kind of, you know, off the cuff in conversation mentioned, you know, what, you know, you and Marcel are building and what GrowthX is.
And, you I was kind of more than happy to just make the connection.
So that's, I have no real agenda, just wanted to, you know, make the, you know, take the introduction and learn a little bit more about what you guys are doing.
But yeah, that's, that's, that's the background from my side.
I've been with the fund since, you know, November 2021, which is crazy.
Moved down to New York, up from Boston.
I'm from there originally, went to Amherst College, was an econ major.
I actually played football there myself.
So, you know, equally pretty excited for, you know, for, for the season to start.
But yeah, it's great to meet you.
Yeah, super cool.
As for myself, I'm the first sales hire here at GrowthX.
So I'm their founding account executive, been here for a month, doing more in a month here than I've done.
Anywhere else by far, but that's a good thing, right?
The demand is crazy and our sales network and expertise in this space is something else.
It sounds like, I mean, definitely you just want to get to know like what we do and how we work.
But are you also thinking about this in terms of like the portfolio companies that you guys have and potential opportunities for them to benefit?
Is that another piece?
I didn't even really, I didn't know, you know, I was just more kind of like making a connection and kind of trusting Justin.
I didn't have like a set agenda.
I definitely want to learn like more about what GrowthX is because Justin was like, yeah, they're a growth agency, but they use AI, but they just raise money and everything.
And, you know, he just had good things to say.
So I'm happy to kind of hear more about it and figure out how we can work together if there's a path there.
Perfect.
Yeah.
Let me start with the overview and give you as much context as I can.
So GrowthX, like think of us as like services as software in a way.
Right?
We're blending the quality of services with the speed and scalability of AI and software.
And what that looks like is GrowthX is an end-to-end partner for our clients from coming up with the initial content strategy around SEO and now GEO to creating the content.

And we can do this at scale because we built AI workflows into our internal platform with experts in the loop, all the way to publishing the content in clients' CMSs and doing all of the LLM tracking and visibility.
So high level, that makes sense.
Anything you want to just like quickly dive deeper into?
I'm going to keep going, but see how you're feeling so far.
No, that makes sense.
Awesome.
So the experts, right?
It's Marcel, and it's also our team that is client-facing.
So every client gets a managing director, managing editor, and content manager.
When you think about like who these folks are, our chief content...
Officer ran TechCrunch for 10 years.
We've got, you know, managing editors who like built the G2.com site, managing directors who ran SEO for TripAdvisor and Fanatics.
If you think about like where we're at in terms of scale, we just crossed 10 million in ARR.
We raised a Series A 12 mil from Madrona and Marcel's working on the Series B fundraising right now.
And we've got over 70 clients that benefit from GrowthX.
You can see a lot of the logos on this slide right here.
So, yeah.
One of the things that was thinking, and sorry to interrupt, I mean, first of all, congratulations.
That's awesome.
What is a good company look like for you guys?
Like, you know, like what makes somebody a better candidate than others, essentially?
Yeah, for sure.
I think in terms of size, like we're not typically trying to work with.
Companies who have no domain authority at all, like just stood up their website.

Although we have like taken on some clients that are at that stage, but like philosophically, I think it's like we are looking for clients who believe in driving organic growth through content, right?
They believe in like the returns that content can provide and they don't want to spend so much money on paid channels, like renting their audience's attention.

They understand that like there's a need to scale content in this world where instead of just like targeting keywords in Google, people are having like their entire first sales conversation in a chat GPT context window.

And there's such like a long tail of like ways that someone could interact with an LLM that, you the only way to really show up is to be the answer to that massive variety of questions and ways that someone might prompt, right?
And, and, and a chat GPT or similar type of tool is that.
Answer your question, Will?
It does.
And are you guys like, do you work hand in hand with like a CRO, with like a head of sales, with like a founder, I guess?
Let's take, I think I saw Hockey Stack on that, you know, as one of the logos.
Like, so they raised a Series A.
I remember them coming out of, you know, coming out of YC.
Like, when do you engage with them?
Because, you know, I asked, we look at Series A companies, we look at C companies.
So like, is there a certain point where it's like, oh, you know, this is, this is a good, this is a good time to, to think about GrowthX here?
Yeah, for sure.
I would say, I mean, Hockey Stack, right?
Like, we just started working with them.
Their CRO is like the main point of contact.
It's usually the person who's running marketing.
Or if it's seed in Series A, it's like a lot of times that founder.
I would say like, you're starting to get some traction with your website and you need to scale content.
And you don't want to go out and like build a massive team, right?
Like hiring editors, writers, freelancers.
You want like a partner who can do more than just SEO.
Strategy and actually help with execution.
That timing might be different for each company, but we are like working with a ton of Series A startups.
Got it.
Okay.
That's really helpful.
So getting some traction on the website, don't want to like what, build, like hire like a marketer or something where it's like, hey, how can we kind of optimize this?
Like you guys come in and start juicing that, you know, that profile.
Exactly.
Like want to stay lean, want to be able to do like more with AI, but also want to like keep the quality really high in terms of like what you guys are publishing and posting on your website.

So like to give some examples and then tell you a little bit more about like how the content gets created, like town.com is a great example because they are seed stage and we've like basically built their entire blog and they are an accounting software company.
So when you think about the content that we're posting, the accuracy is like so important because you can't.
Give bad tax advice or accounting advice.
So anything that you see on their blog was created by us.
And a lot of this just comes down to the two-phased approach with how we work.
The first phase, it's an eight-week engagement that we call a strategy sprint.
And then we move into phase two, which is a 12-month growth execution.
And so what we're doing in the strategy sprint is we're really trying to create these artifacts that will feed the AI workflows with the best context.
Because the content we create, if we're leveraging AI so much, it's only going to be as effective as the context that we give it.
So company context, I mean, this is pretty straightforward, makes sense.
We're going deep into what's the elevator pitch?
Who are the ideal customers?
What are those personas?
And then for Engine that really wanted to target specific verticals across their personas, as we made these industry-specific...

Specific personas where, you know, when you think about what I was saying earlier, like wanting to capture what that ICP cares about and like what they might interface with ChatGPT around, this is like super deep, fears and uncertainties, motivations, how to win their trust, how to annoy their trust, what they care about, what's their pain points.
And so like all of this is feeding the content that gets generated.
And when you think about like the content that's going to work for, you know, GEO, for LLMs, a lot of it just comes down to, is it well-researched?
Is it comprehensive?
Is it fresh?
So we'll do refreshes with a lot more established companies that have content that, you know, they just haven't touched in like a year plus.
And that's what you'll see like when you look into the workflows that create a piece like this that we did for AugmentCode.
So, you know, AugmentCode, when they got to GrowthX, they weren't having a ton of...
But they were at like 5,000 organic traffic.
Fast forward, you know, six months working with us, they're like 5 to 10x that monthly traffic.
And so like, that's the outcomes that we're driving, right?
It's just more traffic from like the ICPs that these companies care about.
But the way that we would actually create a piece like this, you know, it's not just like the AI, there's the experts in the loop who are monitoring this at every single stage.
And, you know, we'll feed these artifacts into specific pipelines.
So like, there are some that are comparison articles, others that have an image with the article.

For this one in particular, 13 best coding AI tools for complex code bases, you'll see that in every step, like there's an output where we generate the brief, we do deep research on what we're going to write about, we create the draft of the article.
And this is like the internal platform that our team uses.
So, you if there's anything in this.
They want to change, you know, they have the ability to, like, work directly with the content and, you know, make those changes through, like, a live editing tool here.
So there's no, like, copy and pasting or anything like that.
We'll do, you know, fact-checking for the accuracy, cross-linking, because that's, like, rewarded really well.
And then we'll generate the SEO meta tags.
What's cool about this is, like, every step is built in code through our workflow engine.
So if I were to, like, inspect one of these different steps here, you'll see that, like, we're doing so much in each of these steps with different APIs, right?

With different steps inside the step, like fetching keyword traffic, analyzing search results, looking at competitor content, and then using all of that to, like, actually inform the brief that's created.
If you keep going here, like, the research step here that you see, we're generating research questions, and then we're going in and we're performing deep research on each one

One of those questions, validating it with sources, and even for the fact-checking, like we're extracting the draft of the article into passages, we're researching each passage, and then we're going in and making sure that we can verify at a certain confidence interval whether the contents or whether the statement is accurate, and if not, we'll recommend a rewrite.

So it's basically like, you know, Marcel, he spent his career building content engines, and then at DeepGram, he distilled all this thought process into these AI workflows, he just like spent thousands of hours building AI workflows for DeepGram, drove their traffic 24x, increased their revenue by 4x, and then put on a workshop around how he did it, which just like led to the creation of GrowthX.

Because there was just like, all this interest in, you know, one, I don't want to try to figure out how to build these workflows myself, two, I don't want to hire big teams or take the time to like, learn how to leverage these.
And then three, I don't want an agency partner who can only tackle one piece of this puzzle when we need to get to publishing daily.
And so, you know, long-winded, but that is like the story of GrowthX in a nutshell.
No, I mean, that's awesome.
And I appreciate the demo.
mean, it's really cool.
I like kind of how AI is baked in.
And then, you know, also the company still has like a ton of control, I guess.
What's like an average uplift or ROI that your customers see?
Yeah, for sure.
So I would say like, you know, what we're focused on is like building that foundation where they can drive traffic.
And from there, right, like it's what's the next step when you've built their trust in terms of like where they would want to go on your site.
So a lot of like ROI, it's not centered around like we drove revenue X percent for this company.
It's more so, you know, hey, we got their traffic up by.

A hundred to a thousand percent in a six month period, you know, here's all the new opportunities that they have to like convert people who are consuming content that's relevant, like to the personas that they're trying to go after.
We got to like beef up our case studies, no doubt about it.
But that's like the first thing we're focused on is, you know, driving traffic that, you know, you can then build distribution.
So like we've built like a newsletter for some of our clients.
We've done more like programmatic SEO plays.
Ramp is a good example.
Like they've been a client for over a year.
We built like this business expense classifier.
Like it makes sense, right?
Like if someone's looking up an expense category, like Ramp wants to show up in a search like that.
You know, same thing with like their vendor directory.
So like they've got all this proprietary data around spend.
We're able to like create this content and then bring that data directly into each piece.
So.
It's pretty bespoke.
Yeah.

Website content, whether that's case studies, whether that's, you know, anything, it's like you guys are kind of not hitmen, but like, not like mercenaries, but like you're able to kind of attack different projects that ultimately, you know, result in higher traffic and just, you know, are like insightful, you know, insightful pieces of content, basically.
Exactly.

Basically, like in today's marketing world, like you want to show up where people are searching and instead of just showing up in Google, right, it's like showing up in all the places that people are having like this first sales conversation around because when they get to sales, right, like they're able to do so much more on their own.

And so if you don't have like a strong editorial foundation, then, right, like what's the point of these case studies if there's not people landing on your website around the topics, right, the topic authority that you need to generate like mindshare around at the end of the day.
Got it.
No, that's awesome.
And in terms of pricing, how do you guys think about that for the companies?
Yeah, for sure.
So for our strategy sprint, it's a fixed fee.
It's $20,000 is what it starts out with for any of the Series A companies.
That's what they would pay for the strategy sprint.
For the growth execution, it starts at $12,000.
And that's where we would land with a company that would be Series A stage.
And we have some clients who pay us a lot more than that, but we're also doing a lot more than editorial.
So that's why the price tag would be higher.
It would be in lanes outside of just editorial and content refreshes.
Yeah.
I mean, you know, for that, for increased web traffic, that seems like it makes sense, honestly, to me.
Got it.
Okay.
And so.
And so what are the right next steps?

mean, I'm happy to just start, you know, keep you guys in mind for, you know, for kind of relevant companies in our portfolio or ones that I speak to where that are that are kind of in that, you know, starting to inflect, you know, don't have kind of a full team, but want to, you know, want to, you know, put put or pour fuel on the fire, if you will.
Yeah, for sure.

I think that would be the best thing to do, right, is when you have a need with one of your portfolio companies where they need an agency partner that can do much more, you know, when they need to be able to do something quick with content, we would probably be the best fit there.

And I guess from like any sort of investing or fundraising perspective, I would defer to Marcel and would just, you know, encourage you to like, let him know if there was any interest in having a conversation in that capacity.
Got it.
No, I appreciate that.
All right.
Well, yeah, look, look, tell him I really enjoyed the conversation and, you know, happy to happy to chat with him.
And do a follow up.
But yeah, look, let's see if I can't, if I can't bring you some business.
Yeah, that sounds great, Bo.
And sorry that he didn't join.
He, before this meeting, had like a meeting with our biggest customer right now.
So I'm, I'm guessing that one went long and you got stuck with me today.
But I do feel like I understand like what we do.
So I think I can at least communicate that to you.
Yeah.
No, absolutely.
I appreciate it.
But I don't want to, I don't want to hold you up.

I think so too.

I will see.

I mean, Texas is going to be pissed from last year.
I think they have more guys coming back.

Tyler, great to meet you.
we'll be in touch.
